title: [SPRINT-1] SP01-T01: Project Scaffolding & Monorepo Setup
body:
## Task: Project Scaffolding & Monorepo Setup
**Sprint:** 1 (Week 1-2) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** DevOps
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead
**Depends On:** None (first task)

---

### Workflow: Assign → Tests → Develop → Verify

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```typescript
// apps/web/__tests__/setup.test.ts
describe('Project Setup', () => {
  it('should have Next.js app at /health');
  it('should have Tailwind CSS configured');
  it('should have shared types importable');
  it('should build all packages via turbo build');
  it('should pass typecheck via turbo typecheck');
});
```
```python
# apps/api/tests/test_health.py
def test_health_endpoint(client):
    response = client.get('/health')
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()['status'] == 'healthy'
```

### Step 2: Development Prompt (Cursor/Claude Code)
```
Create a Turborepo monorepo with:
- apps/web: Next.js 14 + App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
- apps/api: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Alembic + structlog
- packages/shared-types: shared TS types
- packages/ui: shared component lib
- Docker Compose: PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7, LocalStack
- GitHub Actions CI: lint → typecheck → test → build
- Pre-commit hooks via husky + lint-staged
```

### Step 3: Verify
- [ ] `turbo build` succeeds
- [ ] `turbo test` — all setup tests pass
- [ ] `docker compose up` starts all services
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI pipeline green
- [ ] PR merged to develop

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All tests from Step 1 passing
- [ ] Docker Compose starts PostgreSQL + Redis + LocalStack
- [ ] CI pipeline runs on PR creation
- [ ] README updated with setup instructions

title: [SPRINT-1] SP01-T02: Database Schema & Multi-Tenant RLS
body:
## Task: Database Schema & Multi-Tenant RLS
**Sprint:** 1 (Week 1-2) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Backend
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead
**Depends On:** SP01-T01

---

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```python
class TestMultiTenantRLS:
    def test_tenant_isolation_blocks_cross_tenant_read(self, db, tenant_a, tenant_b):
        """Tenant A cannot read Tenant B's data"""
    def test_tenant_isolation_blocks_cross_tenant_write(self, db, tenant_a, tenant_b):
        """Tenant A cannot insert with Tenant B's ID"""
    def test_rls_applies_to_all_tables(self, db):
        """Every table with tenant_id has an RLS policy"""
    def test_audit_log_captures_changes(self, db, tenant_a):
        """INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE logged to audit_log"""
    def test_migration_upgrade_downgrade(self, alembic_runner):
        """Migrations apply and rollback cleanly"""
```

### Step 2: Development Prompt
```
Create PostgreSQL schema with Alembic migrations:
Tables: tenants, users, amazon_connections, agent_actions, approval_queue, notification_log
RLS on all tables: USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant')::uuid)
set_tenant_context() function, audit trigger, pgvector extension
SQLAlchemy 2.0 mapped classes with type hints
```

### Step 3: Verify
- [ ] All 5 RLS unit tests passing
- [ ] Zero cross-tenant data leakage
- [ ] Migrations up/down clean
- [ ] Audit log captures all mutations

### Definition of Done
- [ ] ≥90% test coverage on models/
- [ ] RLS verified with two test tenants
- [ ] PR reviewed and merged

title: [SPRINT-1] SP01-T03: Authentication System (Backend + Frontend)
body:
## Task: Authentication System
**Sprint:** 1 (Week 1-2) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Full-Stack
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead + Frontend Dev
**Depends On:** SP01-T01, SP01-T02

---

### Step 1: Mockup (Stitch)
```
Design login and signup pages for 'Seller Autopilot'.
Login: centered card, email/password, Remember me, SSO buttons (Google/Apple), Forgot password link.
Signup: name, email, password with strength bar, company name, terms checkbox.
Style: navy #1B3A5C accents, white cards, Inter font. Mobile responsive.
```

### Step 2: Write Tests FIRST
**Frontend (Vitest + RTL):**
```
LoginForm: renders fields, validates empty email, validates format, validates short password,
calls onSubmit with valid data, shows loading state, displays server error
SignupForm: shows strength indicator, requires terms, validates all fields
```
**Backend (pytest):**
```
test_signup_creates_tenant_and_user (201)
test_signup_rejects_duplicate_email (409)
test_login_returns_jwt_tokens (200)
test_login_rejects_wrong_password (401)
test_protected_endpoint_requires_auth (401)
test_protected_endpoint_with_valid_token (200)
test_token_refresh (200)
test_tenant_context_set_from_jwt
```
**E2E (Playwright):**
```
test_signup_login_dashboard_flow: signup → onboarding redirect → login → dashboard
test_unauthorized_redirect: /dashboard → redirect to /login
```

### Step 3: Frontend Generation (v0)
```
Create LoginPage and SignupPage with shadcn/ui, react-hook-form, zod validation.
LoginPage: email, password, Remember me checkbox, Sign In button with loading, SSO buttons.
SignupPage: name, email, password with strength bar, company, terms checkbox.
```

### Step 4: Backend Implementation (Cursor)
```
FastAPI auth endpoints: POST /auth/signup, POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh, GET /me
JWT with tenant_id in claims, bcrypt password hashing, Pydantic schemas.
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All frontend unit tests passing (≥80% coverage)
- [ ] All backend unit tests passing (≥90% coverage)
- [ ] E2E auth flow passing in Playwright
- [ ] JWT contains tenant_id, RLS context set automatically
- [ ] PR reviewed and merged

title: [SPRINT-2] SP02-T01: API Gateway Middleware Stack
body:
## Task: API Gateway Middleware Stack
**Sprint:** 2 (Week 3-4) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Backend
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead
**Depends On:** SP01-T02, SP01-T03

---

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```python
TestTenantContextMiddleware:
  test_sets_tenant_from_jwt
  test_rejects_request_without_token (401)
  test_rejects_expired_token (401)

TestRateLimitMiddleware:
  test_allows_requests_within_limit
  test_blocks_requests_exceeding_limit (429 + Retry-After)
  test_rate_limits_are_per_tenant
  test_limits_vary_by_subscription_tier

TestErrorHandler:
  test_returns_standardized_error_json
  test_includes_request_id
  test_no_stack_trace_in_production
```

### Step 2: Development Prompt
```
Build FastAPI middleware: TenantContextMiddleware (JWT→RLS),
RateLimitMiddleware (Redis, per-tenant, tier-based limits),
RequestLoggingMiddleware (structlog JSON), ErrorHandlerMiddleware (standardized errors),
CORSMiddleware. Plus get_current_tenant() dependency.
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All middleware tests passing
- [ ] Rate limiting verified per-tenant with Redis
- [ ] Structured JSON logs in CloudWatch format
- [ ] Standardized error responses across all endpoints

title: [SPRINT-2] SP02-T02: Design Token System & Core Component Library
body:
## Task: Design Tokens & Core Components
**Sprint:** 2 (Week 3-4) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Frontend
**Assignee Role:** Frontend Lead
**Depends On:** SP01-T01

---

### Step 1: Design Exploration (Stitch — FREE)
```
Generate a design system reference sheet for 'Seller Autopilot' SaaS dashboard.
Show: color palette (navy primary, 6 shades each + agent accent colors),
typography (Inter, 7 sizes), button variants (primary/secondary/outline/ghost/danger × sm/md/lg × states),
form inputs (text/select/checkbox/toggle with states), badges, stat cards, card components.
Light and dark mode variants.
```

### Step 2: Write Tests FIRST
```typescript
StatCard: renders value/title, green arrow for up trend, red for down, skeleton when loading
AgentStatusBadge: renders agent name, correct status dot color, pulse on active
ApprovalCard: renders description, calls onApprove/onReject, shows confidence, expands reasoning
DataTable: renders all rows, sorts by column click, filters with search, empty state, loading skeleton
```

### Step 3: Component Generation (v0)
```
Generate shadcn/ui components: StatCard, AgentStatusBadge, ApprovalCard, DataTable (generic with
@tanstack/react-table), all with TypeScript, forwardRef, keyboard accessible.
```

### Step 4: Build Tailwind Config + Storybook
- Extended theme with agent colors, animation classes
- Storybook stories for every component with controls

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All component unit tests passing
- [ ] Storybook running with all components documented
- [ ] Dark mode working for all components
- [ ] Accessibility audit passing (axe-core)

title: [SPRINT-2] SP02-T03: Event Bus & Notification Infrastructure
body:
## Task: Event Bus & Notification Infrastructure
**Sprint:** 2 (Week 3-4) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Backend
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead
**Depends On:** SP01-T01, SP01-T02

---

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```python
TestEventBus:
  test_publishes_event_to_sqs(self): event published, received by consumer
  test_routes_event_to_correct_tenant_queue(self): tenant_a event not in tenant_b queue
  test_dead_letter_queue_on_processing_failure(self): failed events go to DLQ
  test_idempotent_processing(self): same event processed only once
  test_event_schema_validation(self): invalid schema rejected

TestWebSocketManager:
  test_connects_with_valid_token(self)
  test_rejects_without_auth(self)
  test_broadcasts_to_tenant_only(self): tenant_a events not sent to tenant_b
  test_reconnects_after_disconnect(self)
```

### Step 2: Development Prompt
```
Build event bus with SQS (LocalStack for dev) + EventBridge:
- EventPublisher: publish typed events (AgentActionEvent, InventoryAlertEvent, PriceChangeEvent)
- EventConsumer: per-tenant SQS FIFO queues, idempotent processing, DLQ
- WebSocketManager: FastAPI WebSocket endpoint, JWT auth, per-tenant channels,
  heartbeat, auto-reconnect support
- Event schemas with Pydantic: type, tenant_id, timestamp, payload
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Event publishing → consumption tested end-to-end
- [ ] WebSocket connects, authenticates, receives tenant-scoped events
- [ ] DLQ captures failed events
- [ ] Idempotency verified

title: [SPRINT-2] SP02-T04: WebSocket Integration & Frontend Real-Time Hook
[SPRINT-3] SP03-T01: Amazon SP-API OAuth Connection Flow
body:
## Task: Amazon SP-API OAuth Connection
**Sprint:** 3 (Week 5-6) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Full-Stack Integration
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead + Frontend Dev
**Depends On:** SP01-T03, SP02-T01

---

### Step 1: Mockup (Stitch)
```
Design an Amazon account connection wizard (3 steps) for a SaaS onboarding flow:
Step 1: 'Connect Seller Central' — explanation of permissions, 'Connect' button with Amazon logo
Step 2: 'Connecting...' — loading state with checkmarks appearing (API access ✓, Marketplace ✓)
Step 3: 'Connected!' — success state showing marketplace name, seller ID, green check
Also show a 'Connected Accounts' settings page with connection status cards per marketplace.
```

### Step 2: Write Tests FIRST
```python
TestAmazonOAuth:
  test_generates_oauth_url_with_correct_params(self)
  test_exchanges_auth_code_for_tokens(self)
  test_stores_encrypted_refresh_token(self)
  test_rejects_invalid_auth_code(self)
  test_handles_oauth_state_mismatch(self)
  test_validates_marketplace_connectivity(self)
  test_token_refresh_before_expiry(self)
  test_revoke_connection_deletes_tokens(self)
```
```typescript
OnboardingWizard:
  it('shows Connect Seller Central step first');
  it('opens Amazon OAuth in popup on click');
  it('shows loading state during connection');
  it('shows success with marketplace details');
  it('handles OAuth error gracefully');
  it('stores connection and advances to next step');
ConnectedAccountsPage:
  it('shows all connected marketplaces with status');
  it('allows disconnecting a marketplace');
  it('shows reconnect button for expired connections');
```

### Step 3: Implementation
**Backend:** LWA OAuth 2.0 flow, token encryption with Fernet, amazon_connections table CRUD
**Frontend:** Onboarding wizard component, OAuth popup handler, connected accounts settings page

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Full OAuth flow: initiate → Amazon login → callback → tokens stored encrypted
- [ ] Refresh token auto-renewal tested
- [ ] Connection status visible in settings
- [ ] E2E: onboarding wizard connects successfully

title: [SPRINT-3] SP03-T02: SP-API Connector Service
body:
## Task: SP-API Connector Service
**Sprint:** 3 (Week 5-6) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Backend Integration
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead
**Depends On:** SP03-T01

---

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```python
TestSPAPIConnector:
  test_auto_refreshes_expired_token
  test_caches_stable_data_4_hours
  test_caches_volatile_data_5_minutes
  test_rate_limit_adaptive_throttle
  test_retries_on_429_with_backoff
  test_no_retry_on_400
  test_circuit_breaker_opens_on_failures
  test_tenant_isolation_in_api_calls
  test_handles_pagination_automatically
  test_logs_api_calls_for_billing
  test_sandbox_mode_uses_sandbox_endpoint
```

### Step 2: Development Prompt
```
Build SPAPIConnector class: LWA token management (encrypted per-tenant),
adaptive rate limiting (Redis), response caching (stable=4h, volatile=5min),
retry with backoff (max 3), circuit breaker (50% fail→30s pause),
auto-pagination, billing-grade API call logging.
Methods: get_listings, get_pricing, get_inventory, get_orders, submit_feed, subscribe_notification.
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All 11 connector tests passing
- [ ] Token refresh works automatically
- [ ] Rate limiting prevents API throttling
- [ ] Circuit breaker tested with failure injection
- [ ] Pagination handles 150+ items across 3 pages

title: [SPRINT-4] SP04-T01: Listing Agent — AI Content Generation
body:
## Task: Listing Agent v1 — AI Content Generation
**Sprint:** 4 (Week 7-8) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Backend Agent
**Assignee Role:** AI/Agent Developer
**Depends On:** SP03-T02

---

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```python
TestListingAgent:
  test_generates_title_under_200_chars
  test_generates_exactly_5_bullet_points
  test_bullet_points_start_with_caps_benefit
  test_search_terms_under_250_bytes
  test_search_terms_no_title_duplicates
  test_includes_target_keywords_in_content
  test_returns_seo_score_0_to_100
  test_validates_against_product_type_schema
  test_proposed_listing_requires_approval
  test_analyzes_competitors_when_provided
  test_respects_brand_voice_setting
  test_generates_backend_search_terms
  test_handles_variation_listings
```

### Step 2: Development Prompt
```
Build ListingAgent class using Claude API for content generation.
generate_listing(input) → ProposedListing:
  1. Fetch product type definition from SP-API
  2. Analyze competitor ASINs if provided
  3. Generate with Claude: title (≤200), 5 CAPS bullets, description (≤2000), search terms (≤250 bytes)
  4. Validate against schema, calculate SEO score
  5. Return as proposed action (requires human approval)

analyze_listing_quality(asin) → QualityReport
handle_listing_issue(notification) → AgentAction
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All 13 agent tests passing
- [ ] Generated listings meet ALL Amazon constraints
- [ ] SEO scoring algorithm validated
- [ ] Proposed listings saved to agent_actions table
- [ ] Integration test: generate → save → retrieve from API

title: [SPRINT-4] SP04-T02: Listing Management UI (Catalog + Editor)
[SPRINT-4] SP04-T03: Pricing Agent v1 + Real-Time Repricing
body:
## Task: Pricing Agent v1 — Competitor Monitoring & Repricing
**Sprint:** 4 (Week 7-8) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Backend Agent
**Assignee Role:** AI/Agent Developer
**Depends On:** SP03-T02, SP02-T03

---

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```python
TestPricingAgent:
  test_subscribes_to_any_offer_changed_notification
  test_detects_competitor_price_drop
  test_calculates_true_cost_per_unit (product + FBA + referral + shipping + ad spend)
  test_enforces_minimum_price_floor
  test_enforces_maximum_price_ceiling
  test_reprices_within_5_minutes_of_change
  test_game_theory_avoids_race_to_bottom
  test_tracks_buy_box_win_rate
  test_surge_pricing_on_high_demand
  test_markdown_for_slow_inventory
  test_proposes_large_changes_for_approval (>10% change)
  test_auto_executes_small_changes (<5% change)
  test_publishes_price_not_competitive_event

TestPricingCalculator:
  test_calculates_fba_fees_correctly
  test_calculates_referral_fee_by_category
  test_calculates_net_margin_after_all_fees
  test_returns_zero_margin_when_at_break_even
```

### Step 2: Development Prompt
```
Build PricingAgent class:
- Subscribes to ANY_OFFER_CHANGED SP-API notifications
- On price change: fetch competitor offers via Product Pricing API
- Calculate optimal price using game-theory strategy (match/undercut/premium)
- Factor in: FBA fees + referral fees + ad spend + target margin
- Auto-execute small changes (<5%), propose large changes for approval
- Track Buy Box win rate per ASIN
- Publish PRICE_NOT_COMPETITIVE event to event bus for Advertising Agent

Also build PricingCalculator: Amazon fee calculator (FBA, referral, closing)
using Product Fees API or cached fee tables.
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All 17 pricing tests passing
- [ ] Notification handler processes price changes < 5 seconds
- [ ] Fee calculations match Amazon's Seller Central values
- [ ] Large price changes queued for approval
- [ ] Cross-agent event published on non-competitive price

title: [SPRINT-4] SP04-T04: Pricing Dashboard + Buy Box Tracker UI
body:
## Task: Pricing Dashboard UI
**Sprint:** 4 (Week 7-8) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Frontend
**Assignee Role:** Frontend Dev
**Depends On:** SP02-T02, SP04-T03

---

### Step 1: Mockup (Stitch)
```
Design a pricing management dashboard for Amazon sellers:
- Buy Box summary cards: overall win rate %, ASINs won/lost/ineligible, top improvements
- Pricing table: image, ASIN, your price, Buy Box price, lowest competitor, # sellers,
  margin %, last repriced, AI status badge. Color-coded when your price ≠ Buy Box.
  Inline price edit with margin recalculation.
- Price history chart (per ASIN): your price, Buy Box, top 3 competitors over time.
  AI reprice events annotated. Sales velocity overlay.
- Pricing rules panel: min/max price, target margin, strategy selector, pause toggle.
Professional data-heavy layout, dark mode option.
```

### Step 2: Write Tests FIRST
```typescript
BuyBoxSummary:
  it('shows overall win rate percentage');
  it('shows won/lost/ineligible counts');
  it('shows trend arrow for win rate change');

PricingTable:
  it('renders all pricing columns');
  it('highlights rows where price ≠ Buy Box');
  it('allows inline price editing');
  it('recalculates margin on price change');
  it('shows AI repricing status badge');

PriceHistoryChart:
  it('renders multi-line chart with your price + competitors');
  it('shows annotation markers for AI reprice events');
  it('toggles between 7d/30d/90d time ranges');

PricingRulesForm:
  it('saves min/max price per ASIN');
  it('validates min < max');
  it('toggles pause repricing');
```

### Step 3: Generate Components (v0)
```
Generate: BuyBoxSummaryCards, PricingDataTable (with inline editing),
PriceHistoryChart (Recharts multi-line with annotations), PricingRulesForm.
All shadcn/ui, TypeScript, responsive.
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All UI tests passing
- [ ] Dashboard connected to pricing API endpoints
- [ ] Real-time price updates via WebSocket
- [ ] Inline editing triggers repricing agent
- [ ] E2E: view prices → edit → verify update

title: [SPRINT-4] SP04-T05: Human Approval Workflow Engine
body:
## Task: Human Approval Workflow Engine
**Sprint:** 4 (Week 7-8) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Full-Stack
**Assignee Role:** Backend Lead + Frontend Dev
**Depends On:** SP01-T02, SP02-T02, SP02-T03

---

### Step 1: Write Tests FIRST
```python
TestApprovalWorkflow:
  test_agent_proposal_creates_approval_item
  test_approve_triggers_agent_execution
  test_reject_marks_action_rejected
  test_auto_approve_low_risk_actions
  test_auto_reject_on_timeout
  test_batch_approve_similar_actions
  test_approval_requires_authorized_role (owner/admin only)
  test_approval_logs_audit_trail
  test_notification_sent_on_new_approval
  test_priority_ordering_in_queue
```
```typescript
ApprovalQueue component:
  it('renders pending approvals sorted by priority');
  it('shows count badge matching pending count');
  it('approve button calls API and removes item');
  it('reject button calls API and removes item');
  it('batch approve selects multiple and approves all');
  it('shows empty state when no pending approvals');
  it('updates in real-time via WebSocket');
  it('expands to show agent reasoning');
```

### Step 2: Backend Prompt
```
Build approval workflow engine:
- ApprovalService: create_approval(agent_action_id), approve(id, user_id),
  reject(id, user_id, reason), batch_approve(ids, user_id)
- Auto-approve rules engine: configurable per agent, per action type, per risk level
- Timeout handler: background job checks expired approvals
- Notification integration: publish APPROVAL_REQUESTED event
- Audit: full trail in agent_actions table

FastAPI endpoints: GET /approvals (paginated, filterable), POST /approvals/{id}/approve,
POST /approvals/{id}/reject, POST /approvals/batch-approve
```

### Step 3: Frontend Prompt (v0)
```
Generate ApprovalQueue component: list of ApprovalCards, sorted by priority.
Header with pending count badge and 'Approve All Safe' button.
Each card: agent icon, description, ASINs, impact, confidence, approve/reject buttons.
Expandable reasoning. Priority left-border color. Loading and empty states.
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] All approval workflow tests passing
- [ ] Approve → agent executes action verified
- [ ] Auto-approve for configured low-risk actions
- [ ] Real-time queue updates via WebSocket
- [ ] Full audit trail in database
- [ ] E2E: agent proposes → seller sees in queue → approves → action executes

title: [SPRINT-4] SP04-T06: Main Dashboard — Command Center
body:
## Task: Main Dashboard Layout
**Sprint:** 4 (Week 7-8) | **Priority:** P0 | **Type:** Frontend
**Assignee Role:** Frontend Lead
**Depends On:** SP02-T02, SP02-T04, SP04-T05

---

### Step 1: Full Prototype (Lovable)
```
Build a SaaS dashboard for 'Seller Autopilot' Amazon automation platform:
- Collapsible sidebar: Dashboard, Listings, Inventory, Advertising, Pricing, Analytics, Settings
- Top bar: marketplace selector dropdown with flags, Cmd+K search, notification bell, profile menu
- Main content: 3-column widget grid with:
  * Revenue stat card: today's revenue + sparkline + trend
  * Profit stat card: estimated profit + margin % + TACoS
  * Agent status bar: 6 agent icons with colored status dots
  * Approval queue: pending count + top 5 items with approve/reject
  * Inventory alerts: ASINs < 14 days stock
  * Ad performance: spend, ACoS, ROAS mini cards
  * Listing health: active/inactive/suppressed counts
  * Activity feed: scrolling real-time agent action log
React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui. Dark mode. Responsive.
```

### Step 2: Write Tests FIRST
```typescript
Dashboard:
  it('renders all widget sections');
  it('shows revenue and profit stat cards with data');
  it('shows agent status bar with correct statuses');
  it('shows approval queue with pending count');
  it('activity feed updates in real-time via WebSocket');
  it('time range selector changes all widget data');
  it('clicking widget navigates to detail module');
  it('responsive: 3-col desktop, 2-col tablet, 1-col mobile');

Sidebar:
  it('renders all navigation items with icons');
  it('collapses to icon-only on toggle');
  it('highlights active route');
  it('shows badge counts on nav items');

GlobalSearch:
  it('opens on Cmd+K');
  it('searches ASINs, SKUs, campaigns');
  it('shows recent searches');
  it('keyboard navigable results');
```

### Step 3: Production Components (v0)
```
Generate individual components: DashboardLayout, Sidebar, TopBar, GlobalSearchModal,
WidgetGrid (CSS Grid), ActivityFeed (virtual scroll). shadcn/ui, TypeScript.
```

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Dashboard renders with all widgets populated
- [ ] Real-time activity feed working via WebSocket
- [ ] Sidebar navigation works with route highlighting
- [ ] Cmd+K search opens and searches
- [ ] Responsive across all breakpoints
- [ ] All component tests passing
